info.title: REST API - OJS
info.description:
This document describes how to interact with the REST API in [Open Journal Systems](https://pkp.sfu.ca/software/ojs). It is a technical document intended for software developers who wish to build applications to interact with OJS. This document only describes the REST API for OJS. However, similar REST APIs exist for [Open Monograph Press](https://pkp.sfu.ca/software/omp) and [Open Preprint Systems](https://pkp.sfu.ca/software/ops).

View the [Developer Documentation Hub](https://docs.pkp.sfu.ca/dev) to view all documentation for these applications.

tags.name: What's New in 3.5
tags.description:
Read the 3.5 Release Notebook to learn about all the changes introduced in 3.5.
The following major changes have been made to the REST API. - The backend submissions handler (`_submissions`) was extended to support refactored submission listings and review assignments. - API handling has been migrated from the Slim microframework to Laravel. Third-party additions/modifications to the API that made use of Slim features must be migrated to Laravel's routing toolkit. Handler classes have been renamed from `*Handler` to `*Controller`. - Plugins wishing to modify the handling of requests to existing API endpoints should follow a new hook-based pattern. The following schema and behavioral changes also affect API consumers. - Authors can now have multiple institutional affiliations, each optionally linked to a [ROR](https://ror.org/) record. The `affiliation` property on contributors has been replaced with an `affiliations` array of structured affiliation objects. - The `Submission` schema now includes a `reviewerSuggestions` array (read-only) listing reviewer suggestions made at submission time, as well as `issueToBePublished` and (for OJS) `scheduledIn` properties. - The `Galley` `locale` property now accepts BCP 47-style extended locale codes (e.g. `sr_Cyrl@RS`). The following new endpoints have been added. - New endpoints to list and retrieve [sections](#tag/Sections). - New endpoints to retrieve and add records from the [Research Organization Registry (ROR)](#tag/RORs) cache, used to support author affiliations. - New endpoints to manage invitations, including creating, populating, inviting, cancelling, and accepting or declining email-based invitations. In 3.5 this toolset is used for email address change confirmations, one-click reviewer access, and the GDPR-compliant user account creation process. - New endpoints to manage [reviewer suggestions](#tag/Submissions-Reviewer-Suggestions) made by authors during submission. - New endpoints to retrieve [review history](#tag/Reviews), confirm review assignments, export individual reviews as PDF or XML, and submit completed reviews to ORCID. - New endpoints to retrieve, upload, and delete [JATS XML](#tag/Submissions-JATS) source files attached to a submission. - New endpoints to request and revoke [ORCID](#tag/ORCID) verification for individual authors. - New endpoints for authors to retrieve [emails](#tag/Emails) sent to them about their submissions. - New private editorial-UI endpoint at `/submissions/{submissionId}/publications/{publicationId}/_components/{component}` that exposes the [publication form components](https://github.com/pkp/pkp-lib/issues/10336) (metadata, references, title/abstract, identifiers, permission/disclosure, change-language helper, and OJS-only issue and submission-payment forms). - New endpoint `PUT /submissions/{submissionId}/publications/{publicationId}/changeLocale` to change the primary locale of a publication. - The DOI registration agency settings endpoint was relocated from `/contexts/editDoiRegistrationAgencyPlugin` to `PUT /contexts/{contextId}/registrationAgency`.

tags.name: Access
tags.description:
Use the following to access the `/submissions` endpoint when an application is hosted at `https://example.com/`.

```
https://example.com/api/v1/submissions
```

A multi-journal OJS installation must access endpoints from each journal's URL.

```
https://example.com/journalpath/api/v1/submissions
```

Installations that do not use `mod_rewrite` must include `index.php`.

```
https://example.com/index.php/journalpath/api/v1/submissions
```

Administrators can access _some_ endpoints, such as `/contexts`, at a site-wide endpoint that spans all journals.

```
https://example.com/_/api/v1/contexts
```

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description:
Only authenticated users can access the REST API endpoints. Authentication can be done with cookies or by using the API token.

## Cookies

Cookie-based authentication can be used when you are making a request from the same domain name as the application. When a request is made from the user's browser, the browser will send the cookies to the server.

A CSRF token must be sent with every `POST`, `PUT` or `DELETE` request when using cookie-based authentication. Read more about the [CSRF Token](https://docs.pkp.sfu.ca/dev/ui-library/dev/#/pages/api#csrf-token).

## API Token

> The `apiToken` will not validate if the `api_secret_key` setting has not been set in the application's `config.inc.php` file.

Your API token can be found by going to **User Profile > API Key** in the application. Add the API Token in an `Authorization` header with every request made to the API.

```
Authorization: Bearer eyJ0e...6vJU
```

## Connection Issues

 In some Apache configurations, [the Authorization Header may be stripped](https://github.com/pkp/pkp-lib/issues/9320). Additional server configuration may be necessary for Apache to pass the `Authorization` header to PHP.

tags.name: Pagination
tags.description:
Most API endpoints follow the same convention to return subsequent pages of results. The following would return the second "page" of results, if each page contained 30 results.

```
https://example.com/api/v1/submissions?count=30offset=30
```

tags.name: Localization
Links
tags.description:
Some responses will include a `_href` property. The value of this property will be a URL to the full version of this object in the REST API.

```
{
	"_href": "https://example.com/api/v1/submissions/219"
	...
}
```

tags.name: Using Temporary Files
Submissions / Decisions
tags.description: Use these endpoints to get or record editorial decisions on a submission. Editorial decisions include actions like accepting or declining a submission, sending a submission to the next stage, and requesting revisions. Editorial decisions can not be edited or deleted after they are recorded. Learn more about [Decisions](https://docs.pkp.sfu.ca/dev/documentation/en/decisions).

tags.name: Submissions / JATS
tags.description: Endpoints for managing the JATS XML source file associated with a submission's current publication.

tags.name: Submissions / Reviewer Suggestions
tags.description:
Endpoints for managing reviewer suggestions made by an author when submitting their work. Suggestions can be reviewed and approved by editors and converted into reviewer assignments.

The `POST`, `PUT`, and `DELETE` endpoints in this group will only succeed while the submission is still incomplete (i.e. `submissionProgress` is a non-empty string). Once a submission is complete, suggestions become read-only.

tags.name: Invitations
tags.description: Endpoints used to create, send, and respond to email-based invitations. The invitation toolset is used to request an email address change, to provide one-click reviewer access (replacing the older access-keys mechanism), and to support the GDPR-compliant user account creation process. Some endpoints in this group are publicly accessible (using an invitation key) so that invitees can accept, refine, or decline an invitation without authenticating.

tags.description: Only admins and journal managers can access these endpoints.

tags.description: Endpoints to retrieve review round history, confirm review assignments on behalf of a reviewer, export individual review responses as PDF or XML, and submit completed reviews to ORCID.

tags.name: RORs
tags.description: Endpoints to query the local cache of records from the [Research Organization Registry (ROR)](https://ror.org/). These records are used to identify institutions when adding author affiliations.

tags.name: Sections
tags.description: Endpoints to retrieve sections (or series in OMP) configured in the current context. Most authenticated users can access these endpoints.

JatsFile.description: Represents the JATS XML attached to a submission's publication. When the publication has an uploaded JATS XML source file, the response also includes the file's summary properties (those of a `SubmissionFileSummary`).

jatsContent.description: The JATS XML content. May be generated dynamically from publication metadata, or read from an uploaded source file.

isDefaultContent.description: `true` when `jatsContent` was generated dynamically from publication metadata (because no JATS source file has been uploaded); `false` when it was read from an uploaded source file.

loadingContentError.description: Present only if the JATS content could not be loaded. Contains a translated error message.

InvitationUser.description: A user associated with an invitation. Returned for both an existing matched user and the prospective new user built from the invitation payload.

familyName.description: Family name in the user's primary locale.

givenName.description: Given name in the user's primary locale.

affiliation.description: Affiliation in the user's primary locale.

UserRoleAssignmentInvite.description: A user role assignment invitation. Combines the base invitation record with the role-assignment payload (proposed user details, ORCID data and the user groups to be assigned).

contextId.description: The context the invitation belongs to.

inviterId.description: ID of the user who sent the invitation.

userId.description: ID of the invited user, or `null` when the invitation is for a new user.

familyName.description: Localized family name, keyed by locale.

country.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code for the invitee.

username.description: Proposed username for a new user.

sendEmailAddress.description: Email address the invitation is sent to.

userGroupsToAdd.description: The roles (user groups) to assign when the invitation is accepted.

userGroupName.description: Localized name of the user group.

existingUser.description: The matched existing user, or `null` if the invitation is for a new user.

newUser.description: The prospective new user built from the payload, or `null` if an existing user was matched.

example.description: This email is sent when a discussion is created or replied to in the copyediting stage.
example.name: Discussion (Copyediting)

subject.fr_CA: Un message à propos de la revue {$journalName}

body.en:
Dear {$recipientName},

A new submission is ready to be copyedited:

{$submissionId} {$submissionTitle}
{$journalName}

Please follow these steps to complete this task:

  1. 1. Click on the Submission URL below.
  2. 2. Open any files available under Draft Files and edit the files. Use the Copyediting Discussions area if you need to contact the editor(s) or author(s).
  3. 3. Save the copyedited file(s) and upload them to the Copyedited panel.
  4. 4. Use the Copyediting Discussions to notify the editor(s) that all files have been prepared, and that the Production process may begin.

If you are unable to undertake this work at this time or have any questions, please contact me. Thank you for your contribution to this journal.

Kind regards,

{$signature}
body.fr_CA:
{$recipientName},

J'aimerais que vous effectuiez la révision du manuscrit intitulé « {$submissionTitle} » pour la revue {$journalName} à l'aide des étapes suivantes.
1. Cliquer sur l'URL de la soumission ci-dessous.
2. Ouvrir le(s) fichier(s) disponible(s) sous Fichiers des ébauches finales et effectuer votre révision, tout en ajoutant des discussions sur la révision, le cas échéant.
3. Enregistrer le(s) fichier(s) révisé(s) et le(s) téléverser dans la section Version(s) révisée(s).
4. Informer le,la rédacteur-trice que tous les fichiers ont été révisés et que l'étape de production peut débuter.

URL de la revue {$journalName} : {$journalUrl}
URL de la soumission : {$submissionUrl}
Nom d'utilisateur-trice : {$recipientUsername}

get.description:
* Unpublished submissions are only returned for assigned participants, editors and admins.
* `abstract` includes HTML markup.
* `pages` is usually a `string`, but in some cases it may return an array of arrays. Each array contains two items: the start and end page. Example: `[[25, 31], [45, 62]]`.
* `submissionProgress` will be a non-empty string if the submission is incomplete.
* `stages`, `reviewRounds` and `reviewAssignments` will only be returned for participants assigned to the submission.
* `reviewAssignments` only expose user-identifying information for editors and admins.
* `reviewerSuggestions` lists reviewer suggestions made by the author at submission time. This array is read-only here; manage suggestions via the `/submissions/{submissionId}/reviewers/suggestions` endpoints.
* `scheduledIn` is the ID of the issue this submission has been scheduled into, when applicable.
* `issueToBePublished` is a summary of the issue this submission will appear in once it is published.

post.summary: Record a decision for a submission.
post.description:
Record a new editorial decision for this submission. This endpoint is configured to support customizable actions when recording a decision. Each decision supports pre-defined actions, but plugins can extend each decision to take additional actions or create new decisions.

Most of the built-in decisions use one or more actions. View the "Request samples" for this endpoint to see which decisions use which actions. Most decisions use one or more of the following types:

| Action | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Email | Details about the email message, recipients and attachments. If the example request body includes an empty `recipients` array, that means the decision does not allow the recipients to be modified. It is configured to always send to the same recipients. For example, most emails to notify authors will automatically be sent to the assigned authors and co-authors, depending on how the journal has configured their email notification settings. |
| Form | A form payload that includes the action `id` and any input data from the form. |

A custom decision is not restricted to these types. Any data can be sent through the `actions` property and handled on the server. Learn more about [Decisions](https://docs.pkp.sfu.ca/dev/documentation/en/decisions).

get.description:
This will return all of a submission's files that the user is allowed to access, except review attachments and discussion files. A user is allowed to access different file stages depending on their stage assignment.
* Managers, subeditors and assistants can access all file stages if they are assigned to the matching workflow stage. For example, an assistant assigned to the copyediting stage can access files in `SUBMISSION_FILE_COPYEDIT` and `SUBMISSION_FILE_FINAL`.
* Authors are restricted to the `SUBMISSION_FILE_SUBMISSION`, `SUBMISSION_FILE_REVIEW_REVISION`, `SUBMISSION_FILE_COPYEDIT` and `SUBMISSION_FILE_PROOF` file stages. For example, an author with access to the copyediting stage will have access to `SUBMISSION_FILE_COPYEDIT` (copyedited files) but not `SUBMISSION_FILE_FINAL` (draft files).
* Reviewers and readers are not allowed to access this endpoint.
* Managers who are not assigned to the submission are allowed to access all file stages as though they are assigned to every workflow stage.

|`api.submissionFiles.403.unauthorizedFileStageId`|You are not allowed to access files in one or more of the `fileStages` requested.|
|`api.submissionFiles.403.unauthorizedReviewRound`|You are not allowed to access files in one or more of the `reviewRoundIds` requested.

get.summary: Get a configured publication form component used by the editorial UI.
get.description:
Returns the JSON configuration for one of the publication-edit forms rendered by the editorial UI. The available components are:

- `metadata` – the publication metadata form
- `reference` – the citations / references form
- `titleAbstract` – the title and abstract form
- `changeLanguageMetadata` – the change-primary-language helper used to migrate metadata across locales
- `identifier` – the publication identifiers form (URL path, etc.)
- `permissionDisclosure` – the licensing / permissions / copyright form
- `issue` – OJS only: the issue-entry form (issue, section, cover image, pages, publication date)
- `submissionPayment` – OJS only: the submission payment form

These endpoints are part of the private editorial UI and are not intended for third-party use. The exact response shape depends on the requested component and follows the [PKP Form Component](https://docs.pkp.sfu.ca/dev/ui-library/dev/#/component/Form) configuration format.

get.summary: Get the review history for a review round.
get.description: Returns the **calling reviewer's** review-round data for the given submission and round: publication metadata at the time of the review, the review assignment itself, the recommendation, comments, attachments, and the submission files that were under review. The endpoint filters by the authenticated user as the reviewer, so an editor or admin calling it without being personally assigned will receive a 404.

200.description: Aggregated review-round data: publication metadata at the time of review, the review assignment itself, the reviewer's recommendation, viewable and private comments, attachments, and submission files.

The properties of the `ReviewAssignment` schema are returned wrapped in a top-level `_data` object — i.e. the response body looks like `{"_data": { … review assignment properties … }}`. This wrapper is a side-effect of the controller returning the raw data object without schema-mapping.

403.description: You do not have permission to confirm this review assignment.

404.description: The submission or review assignment could not be found.
